source.title: Dynamics of Awake Hippocampal-Prefrontal Replay for Spatial Learning and Memory-Guided Decision Making.

confidence_rationale: Direct measurement of joint hippocampal-prefrontal replay during pause-then-decide epochs, showing that coordinated replay distinguishes correct future paths from alternatives -- which is the candidate-versus-discarded distinction MECH-057b is concerned with. Source quality is very high (Neuron, simultaneous HPC and PFC ensembles, longitudinal learning across the same animals). Mapping fidelity is good: the paper directly addresses the read-out structure (PFC) that MECH-057b implies must receive only promoted candidates.
failure_signatures: If PFC ensembles tracked all replayed paths equally regardless of correctness, the candidacy-gate framing would be weakened.
If learning never produced a forward-replay shift toward correct future paths, the proposal-promotion mechanism would lack a learning signature.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Coordinated awake hippocampal-prefrontal replay distinguishes correct past and future paths from alternative choices, with a learning shift from reverse-replay-based retrospective evaluation to forward-replay-based prospective planning.
mapping.ree_translation: PFC-side readout of hippocampal replay reflects only the trajectories that survive the candidacy gate (correct paths). Wrong/alternative paths are not equally represented downstream, suggesting that the hippocampus emits selectively and that PFC sees the post-gate output. Maps onto MECH-057b's claim that downstream selection (E3 / PFC analog) operates on already-promoted candidates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper measures coordination strength between HPC and PFC, not the gate per se. It is possible PFC itself filters incoming hippocampal content rather than receiving a pre-filtered stream. The MECH-057b claim is location-specific (gate is in hippocampus), which Shin et al. do not directly resolve.
mapping.source_context: Awake rats on a continuous spatial alternation task; simultaneous CA1 + mPFC ensemble recording; longitudinal across learning.

confidence_components.notes: Excellent simultaneous-recording paper. Mapping is good because it directly speaks to the HPC-PFC coupling MECH-057b cites in ARC-028. The location of the gate (HPC vs PFC) remains unresolved by this paper alone.
